assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-30001.md exists with full sub-task content. report.md confirms: '30001 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-30002.md exists with full sub-task content. report.md confirms: '30002 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Action No sub-task created. Minor style feedback that does not affect correctness.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30003 | ... | nit | No sub-task -- minor style feedback about error context message'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Action No sub-task created. This asks for clarification; no code change is requested.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30004 | ... | question | No sub-task -- asks for design clarification about GET behavior for deleted SBOMs'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 21), '## Implementation Notes' (line 24), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 30). subtask-30002.md contains: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 19), '## Implementation Notes' (line 22), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 29). Both sub-tasks have all five required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 8: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md line 8: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. Both contain the exact URL.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md has '## Review Context' (line 15) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File, and the full review comment text quoted. subtask-30002.md has '## Review Context' (line 13) with Comment ID 30002, Author reviewer-a, File, and the full review comment text quoted. Both include the original review comment verbatim.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), sub-task descriptions (subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) exist in the outputs. The report does not mention any code modifications being made. The skill output is purely analytical: classifications, sub-task descriptions, and verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md contains a 'Review Comment Classifications' table listing all four comments with their classifications and actions. This demonstrates all comments were processed before the report was generated.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md states 'Overall: FAIL' and describes outstanding issues. There is no mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR anywhere in the report. The report explicitly lists two code changes required before merge.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains in the main table: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`) with 5 test functions; no modified or deleted test files |'. Also in the Style/Conventions section: '**Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE** Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No modified or deleted test files.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies the comment directly as 'code change request' and does not mention convention upgrade analysis. The report's Style/Conventions section states: '**Convention Upgrade -- N/A** No comments classified as "suggestion" to evaluate for upgrade.' Because comment 30002 was classified as a code change request rather than a suggestion, convention upgrade analysis was skipped entirely. The assertion requires that convention upgrade eligibility IS evaluated for comment 30002, but no such evaluation appears anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30002.md exists with a full sub-task description for the index addition. review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'code change request' with 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' report.md confirms: '30002 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created'. The sub-task was created via direct classification as code change request.
